action: Reduce export targets by 20%; activate currency hedging strategies
description: Sri Lanka's 2022 economic crisis caused severe fuel shortages that disrupted factory operations, transport, and port logistics. Export volumes dropped 18% below seasonal average.

action: Source alternative organic inputs; notify international buyers of lower volumes
description: Government ban on chemical fertilisers caused a sharp drop in tea yields across all elevation zones. High-grown and low-grown estates reported 15-22% yield reductions.

action: Monitor shipping delays; build buffer stock for key buyers (Russia, UAE, UK)
description: Pandemic lockdowns disrupted port operations and reduced export volumes significantly. Container availability dropped and freight costs surged 40% during peak months.

action: Review production capacity and re-evaluate near-term demand forecasts
description: Unexplained dip in monthly export volume — 14% below seasonal average. Possibly linked to delayed auction clearances at the Colombo Tea Auction.

action: Expedite pending shipments to lock in favourable exchange rates
description: Rapid LKR depreciation against USD created a short-term pricing advantage for exporters but introduced uncertainty in forward contract pricing with major buyers.
